text.title: Cloud Run Jobs Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the cloud-run-jobs methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Cloud Run Job tasks MUST be idempotent: running the same task twice with the same `CLOUD_RUN_TASK_INDEX` MUST produce the same result and not corrupt state.
rule.rationale: Cloud Run retries failed tasks up to `max-retries`; non-idempotent tasks cause duplicate writes, double-charged payments, or corrupted state.

rule.statement: Every job MUST set `--task-timeout` explicitly. Default of 10 minutes is unsafe for ETL/report workloads.
rule.rationale: Hitting default timeout silently truncates jobs and emits opaque failures; explicit timeout makes capacity planning deterministic.

rule.statement: Tasks MUST use `CLOUD_RUN_TASK_INDEX` and `CLOUD_RUN_TASK_COUNT` to slice the work set; the last index handles any remainder.
rule.rationale: Without index-based partitioning, every task reprocesses the full set, multiplying cost and producing duplicate output.

rule.statement: Tasks running >30 minutes MUST checkpoint progress to Cloud Storage so retries resume from the checkpoint, not from scratch.
rule.rationale: Without checkpointing a single transient failure forces a full re-execution and quickly exceeds the 168-hour cap on long jobs.

rule.statement: Cloud Scheduler-driven jobs MUST use a dedicated service account with `roles/run.invoker` on the job, not the default compute SA.
rule.rationale: Default compute SA carries broad permissions; a dedicated SA scoped to `run.invoker` keeps the blast radius of credential leaks small.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
